duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Inverted boolean logic in onlyOperator modifier (|| instead of && !) locks out designated operators from all staking management functions
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All seven threats describe the identical code defect: the onlyOperator modifier condition `msg.sender != owner() || operator[msg.sender]` reverts when operator[msg.sender] is true. Same root cause (LiquidRon.sol onlyOperator modifier), same impact (all operator-gated functions inaccessible to operators). TECH-H001 selected for most comprehensive boolean logic walkthrough and full affected function enumeration.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: harvestAndDelegateRewards creates unbacked operatorFeeAmount (phantom fee) since rewards are re-delegated without WRON entering the vault, draining user principal when fetchOperatorFee is called
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All nine threats target the same accounting flaw: harvestAndDelegateRewards increments operatorFeeAmount without depositing WRON to vault, creating unbacked fee liability paid from user deposits via fetchOperatorFee. OPS-H006 describes the same root function's accounting category shift (rewards→staked) which is a direct consequence of this phantom fee mechanism. ECON-H002 selected for clearest economic impact quantification, complete exploit chain description, and CRITICAL severity.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: totalAssets() does not subtract operatorFeeAmount from vault WRON balance after harvest(), artificially inflating share price until fetchOperatorFee() is called
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All ten threats describe the same accounting gap: totalAssets = super.totalAssets() + getTotalStaked() + getTotalRewards() fails to subtract operatorFeeAmount, creating predictable share price inflation between harvest and fee collection. ECON2-H007 and ECON2-H011 describe the same inflation applied to epoch finalization timing — same root cause. TECH2-H003 here refers to the FIRST occurrence with title 'Share Price Inflation via Unaccounted operatorFeeAmount' (the second TECH2-H003 about reentrancy is a different threat, retained as unique). ECON-H003 selected for detailed arbitrage economics and multi-block exploitation analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: LiquidProxy.harvest() calls claimRewards with the full consensus address array inside a loop, resulting in N redundant external calls that waste gas and risk DoS
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All threats target the same loop bug in LiquidProxy.harvest() where claimRewards(_consensusAddrs) passes the full array each iteration. OPS-H004, TECH2-H004, and OPS2-H005 also mention the balance-based accounting issue (covered separately in DUP-005). Both occurrences of TECH2-H004 (first: 'Proxy harvest Uses address(this).balance and Loops'; second: 'Proxy harvest implementation bug') are removed as duplicates. TECH-H004 selected for HIGH severity, comprehensive description of both the loop defect and its DoS implications.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: LiquidProxy.harvest() uses address(this).balance as claimed amount instead of delta tracking, allowing external RON donations via open receive() to inflate harvested amount and operator fees
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three threats describe the same balance-based accounting vulnerability: address(this).balance includes pre-existing RON (from open receive()), which gets counted as harvested rewards, inflating operatorFeeAmount. ECON-H004 selected for detailed fee recipient attack economics and clearest explanation of external donation vector.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: No cancellation mechanism for withdrawal requests — shares transferred to vault via requestWithdrawal are permanently locked if epoch is never finalized
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three describe the missing cancelWithdrawal function and the dependency on finaliseRonRewardsForEpoch for fund recovery. TECH2-H010 here refers to the FIRST occurrence titled 'Withdrawal Requests Cannot Be Cancelled' (the second TECH2-H010 about Escrow deposit path is different, retained as unique). TECH-H005 selected for explicitly connecting this risk to the onlyOperator bug as an amplifying factor.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: redeem(uint256 _epoch) emits WithdrawalClaimed event with current withdrawalEpoch instead of the _epoch parameter being redeemed
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All four describe the identical variable shadowing bug: local `epoch = withdrawalEpoch` used in event emission instead of `_epoch`. TECH2-H007 here refers to the FIRST occurrence titled 'redeem Emits Incorrect Epoch in Event' (the second TECH2-H007 about rounding mismatch is a different vulnerability in DUP-013). TECH-H006 selected for clearest description of the variable naming defect.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Permissionless pruneValidatorList() can remove active validators during transient zero-stake states, deflating totalAssets and enabling share price manipulation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All nine threats describe the same vulnerability: pruneValidatorList has no access control and removes validators based on point-in-time external reads that may be transiently zero. TECH2-H009 here refers to the FIRST occurrence titled 'Unprotected pruneValidatorList Can Remove Active Validators' (the second TECH2-H009 about array length checks is different, retained as DUP-014 representative). TECH2-H008 here refers to the SECOND occurrence titled 'pruneValidatorList can be used to manipulate totalAssets'. TECH-H007 selected for HIGH severity and most comprehensive analysis of the transient state attack window.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: ERC4626 first depositor inflation attack due to missing _decimalsOffset override (defaults to 0), allowing share price manipulation via donation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All eleven threats describe the classic ERC4626 first-depositor inflation attack exploiting the lack of _decimalsOffset override. TECH2-H005 here refers to the FIRST occurrence titled 'First Depositor Inflation Attack' (the second TECH2-H005 about forced receive() deposits is different, retained as unique). TECH-H009 selected for noting the config_dependent deployment script mitigation (10000 ETH seed) while clearly explaining the code-level vulnerability.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: LiquidProxy.undelegateAmount() assumes bulkUndelegate returns exact requested RON immediately, causing revert if staking contract has cooldown or slashing
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All seven threats (including both occurrences of TECH2-H006) describe the same assumption violation: _depositRONTo(vault, totalUndelegated) is called immediately after bulkUndelegate without verifying actual received balance. Both TECH2-H006 occurrences describe this same issue. TECH-H016 selected for HIGH severity, most detailed analysis of Ronin staking behavior assumptions, and explicit mention of both cooldown and slashing scenarios.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: _checkUserCanReceiveRon uses 0-value call that doesn't guarantee non-zero value transfers will succeed, potentially trapping withdrawal request funds
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three describe the same inadequate validation: 0-value call succeeds for contracts that revert on non-zero value, trapping funds in the withdrawal flow. TECH2-H008 here refers to the FIRST occurrence titled '_checkUserCanReceiveRon False Positive Locks Funds'. TECH-H011 selected for most thorough analysis including CEI pattern verification of the redeem function.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Unbounded validator list growth via _tryPushValidator without effective pruning causes gas DoS for totalAssets-dependent operations
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All four describe the same unbounded growth issue: validators are added by delegateAmount/redelegateAmount/_tryPushValidator but ineffectively removed, causing O(validators * proxies) gas growth in totalAssets queries. OPS2-H008 focuses on pruneValidatorList itself becoming DoS'd; same root cause. ECON2-H014 selected for HIGH severity, detailed growth mechanism analysis, and explicit O(validators * proxies) gas cost modeling.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Custom _convertToAssets for epoch redemption uses different rounding offsets (+1/+10^_decimalsOffset) than ERC4626 previewRedeem used at finalization, enabling systematic over/under-payment
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three describe the same rounding divergence between epoch finalization (previewRedeem) and individual claim (_convertToAssets). TECH2-H007 here refers to the SECOND occurrence titled 'Custom epoch redeem conversion uses non-standard offsets'. ECON-H016 focuses on dust locked in Escrow (same rounding root cause). TECH-H018 selected for most comprehensive analysis of the formula divergence and cumulative over-redemption risk.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: delegateAmount/redelegateAmount lack array length consistency checks between _amounts and _consensusAddrs arrays
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe missing require(_amounts.length == _consensusAddrs.length) validation. TECH2-H009 here refers to the SECOND occurrence titled 'delegateAmount/redelegateAmount lack array length consistency checks'. TECH2-H009 selected for covering both delegateAmount and redelegateAmount across both LiquidRon and LiquidProxy.
